summary: Tested file-upload + RAG integration end-to-end. Backend uploads (text/PDF/image-vision), list, delete, mime/size guards, thread cascade delete, and frontend paperclip/attachment-bar flow ALL work. CRITICAL bug: end-to-end RAG fails — even though the local_retrieval agent returns the correct grounded answer (e.g. 'BANANA-PHONE-9472 ... per secret_notes.txt'), the LLM-judge scores it 0/10 while scoring agents that admit 'I cannot see attachments' at 10/10, so the refiner produces a final answer that contradicts the uploaded file. A secondary semantic-cache pollution bug then serves that wrong answer for any near-duplicate question. 10/11 backend tests pass; the 1 failure is the exact user-facing scenario in the spec.

critical.issue: RAG INTEGRATION BROKEN END-TO-END: For question 'What is the secret token in my uploaded file?' with an uploaded text file containing 'BANANA-PHONE-9472', local_retrieval agent CORRECTLY answers with the token and cites the filename, but the judge scores: local_retrieval=0.0, general_llm=10.0, tavily_web=10.0, arxiv_research=9.0. Best=general_llm whose answer is 'I cannot access attachments'. Refiner synthesises 'I am sorry, but I cannot access or view attachments...' which is the OPPOSITE of the grounded answer. Net result: uploads_used event fires, retrieval works, but the user gets told the file does not exist. Likely root cause: judge LLM treats 'reveals secret' as policy violation and scores grounded answer to 0 — judge prompt has no rule that grounded/cited answers should beat 'cannot see file' refusals. Refiner has no override to prefer the only agent that actually used uploaded context.

SEMANTIC CACHE POLLUTION: cache is keyed per-user only, not per-thread and not per-uploads-state. Once a bad answer ('I cannot see your file') is cached for a question, every subsequent run with the SAME question — even after uploading the file — returns the cached bad answer (similarity=1.0, cache_hit=true) and skips the agents entirely. Reproduces 100%: first run gives wrong answer (per bug above), second run returns it from cache without ever consulting the new uploaded_files. Fix: skip cache when thread has uploaded_files, OR include thread_id / upload_count in cache key, OR invalidate user's cache on /api/uploads insert for that thread.

minor.issue: Returns 200 with {'ok': true} instead of REST-idiomatic 204 No Content. Not a blocker; spec calls for 'ok'.

Does not verify the thread belongs to the calling user (filters only by user_id+thread_id). A user passing someone else's thread_id will simply get an empty list, which is safe but silently — consider 404 when thread is not owned by the caller for clarity.

integration_issues.issue: Backend RAG bug above surfaces to user: paperclip→upload→ask flow produces an answer claiming no attachment exists, even though sidebar shows the uploaded file and attachment chip rendered correctly.

action_items: FIX judge or refiner to prefer the local_retrieval answer when uploads_used event fired and that agent actually grounded its answer in the uploaded chunks. Options: (a) add explicit judge rule 'when user has uploads, an answer that cites the uploaded filename must score above any answer claiming the file cannot be seen', (b) hard-prefer local_retrieval in refiner when upload_count>0 and its answer is non-error.

FIX semantic cache to be upload-aware. Either (a) skip cache.search() when upload_count>0 for this thread, (b) include thread_id in the cache key, or (c) invalidate per-user cache on /api/uploads insert.
Optional: tighten judge prompt to forbid penalising grounded answers that contain proper-noun tokens from the user's own uploaded context.
Optional: add ownership check on GET /api/uploads to 404 unknown thread_ids.
critical_code_review_comments: agents/cache.py: SemanticCache is hydrated once per user and never invalidated on uploads — fundamental design gap for a multi-source RAG that includes user uploads as a source.

chat/stream.py L260-287: upload_count>0 emits 'uploads_used' but is not propagated into the cache lookup decision (cache.search runs BEFORE this code). Cache check should be skipped (or keyed differently) when uploads exist on the thread.

chat/stream.py L335-344: refiner prompt says 'Prefer the best-scored answer' with no carve-out for 'best-scored answer contradicts the only agent that used the user's uploaded context'. Combined with judge mis-scoring, this causes total information loss.

uploads/extractors.py describe_image: catches all exceptions and returns string starting with '[Vision unavailable: ...]'; that string still gets persisted as a chunk and indexed, which can pollute retrieval. Consider skipping chunk persistence on vision failure.

uploads/routes.py upload_file: file.read() loads entire body into memory before size check. For oversize protection prefer streaming / spool size check; 16 MB test works but a malicious client can DoS the worker by holding the connection.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, paperclip, file dialog, upload, attachment chip, new-thread sidebar entry all work)

test_credentials: admin@decision-engine.dev / admin123 (seeded). Used as-is, login OK.
seed_data_creation: Test created several threads + uploaded files under the admin account during testing (visible in sidebar as secret_notes.txt, secret_ui_test.txt, cascade.txt etc). These are harmless TEST_ remnants — main agent may clean if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run pytest with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL set: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://multi-source-rag.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_uploads.py -v`. The only failing test (test_stream_with_uploads) asserts the user-facing RAG behavior — final answer must contain the uploaded token. To reproduce the cache bug independently: ask the exact same question twice; second run will return cache_hit=true with the stale bad answer (similarity 1.0). To bypass the cache during dev testing, vary the question wording slightly — then you will see in agent_complete events that local_retrieval has the correct answer but judge mis-scores it 0.0.
rca of the issue: RCA — RAG final-answer failure: (1) /api/ask/stream correctly retrieves user upload chunks via TF-IDF (retrieve_thread_docs) and injects them into local_retrieval's context. (2) local_retrieval LLM correctly answers with the token, citing the filename. (3) LLM-judge in _score_trace assigns score 0.0 to local_retrieval (likely because the judge LLM applies a 'don't reveal secrets/tokens' safety bias) and 10.0 to general_llm whose answer is a refusal. (4) Refiner takes best_index=general_llm and produces a final answer that contradicts retrieval. (5) Result is cached via SemanticCache.add(user_id, question, final_answer); since cache is per-user (not per-thread / not per-upload-state), every subsequent same/near-question is served the polluted answer with cache_hit=true, skipping agents entirely. Mitigation: (a) make cache upload-aware (skip or key by thread when uploads exist), (b) bias judge/refiner to prefer the local_retrieval answer when uploads_used has fired AND that answer references the uploaded filename. Reproduction: 1) login as admin 2) POST /api/uploads with text containing a unique token 3) POST /api/ask/stream {question:'What is the secret token in my uploaded file?', thread_id:<that thread>} -> observe final_answer does not contain the token; observe agent_complete event 0 (local_retrieval) DOES contain it; observe judge_scores best_index != 0.
